citation: Aron, A., Melinat, E., Aron, E. N., Vallone, R. D., & Bator, R. J. (1997). The Experimental Generation of Interpersonal Closeness: A Procedure and Some Preliminary Findings. Personality and Social Psychology Bulletin, 23(4), 363-377. doi:10.1177/0146167297234003
verification: Prompt wording verified verbatim against the Greater Good in Action reproduction maintained by UC Berkeley's Greater Good Science Center (https://ggia.berkeley.edu/practice/36_questions_for_increasing_closeness).
selection_notes: 6 prompts from Aron Set II (medium intimacy) and Set III (high intimacy). All elicit self-disclosure about the subject; paired-exercise prompts excluded as incompatible with single-turn capture. Emotional weight varied across the 6. Conceptually non-overlapping. See docs/hypotheses/2026-05-14_fresh_prompt_confirmation.md §3 for selection criteria.
prompts: If a crystal ball could tell you the truth about yourself, your life, the future, or anything else, what would you want to know?
Is there something that you've dreamed of doing for a long time? Why haven't you done it?
What is your most treasured memory?
What roles do love and affection play in your life?
What, if anything, is too serious to be joked about?
Of all the people in your family, whose death would you find most disturbing? Why?
